title: collinsia parviflora var. grandiflora
intro: Collinsia parviflora var. grandiflora, also known as grand sky pilot, is a variety of flowering plant in the plantain family (Plantaginaceae). It is native to the western United States and is a perennial herb with blue flowers that grow to a height of about 20-30 inches. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and is used to treat various health issues such as colds, fever, and digestive problems. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, and has been used to promote respiratory health, reduce inflammation, and support immune function.
